name: Shield
description: A defensive layer or equipment mechanic that absorbs, blocks, regenerates, or redirects incoming damage.
design_notes: Shield can be an item, regenerating health layer, directional guard, or temporary barrier. It should define coverage, durability, recharge, damage types, shield-break behavior, and how it differs from ordinary block or armor.
design_purpose: Provide a defensive layer that changes how players absorb or avoid damage.
Create tactical decisions around raising, conserving, breaking, or regenerating protection.
Support enemy and weapon identities through shield counters and damage types.
player_fantasy: The player feels protected by a visible defensive layer that can hold under pressure until it breaks.

parameters.name: shield_capacity_points
parameters.description: Damage capacity before shield breaks or depletes.

parameters.name: shield_recharge_delay_ms
parameters.description: Delay after taking damage before recharge begins.

parameters.name: shield_recharge_points_per_second
parameters.description: Recovery rate while recharge is active.

parameters.name: shield_coverage_arc_degrees
parameters.description: Directional coverage if the shield is not omnidirectional.

parameters.name: shield_break_stun_frames
parameters.description: Vulnerability applied when shield capacity reaches zero.

edge_cases: Damage overflow after shield break must decide whether excess damage carries to health.
Damage over time, poison, environmental hazards, and falls need explicit shield interaction.
Shield recharge should pause, reset, or continue during cutscenes, menus, and respawn according to design.
Friendly fire and team shields need ownership and collision filtering rules.
common_bugs: Shield absorbs unblockable or environmental damage because damage type filters are ignored.
Recharge starts every frame after a hit and never respects the delay.
Overflow damage is applied twice when shield breaks.
Shield visuals remain active after capacity reaches zero.
balancing_notes: Regenerating shields encourage cover and disengagement; finite shields encourage resource conservation.
Shield counters should be readable enough that players understand why protection failed.
accessibility_notes: Use clear shield capacity and break feedback through sound, UI, and visual state, not only subtle shader changes.
Avoid high-frequency shield flicker effects without intensity controls.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent shield_capacity, recharge_delay, and damage-type filters on a shield component. Apply shield absorption and overflow-to-health in one damage transaction before updating visuals.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep shield points, recharge timer, and active coverage on the actor state. The damage resolver checks shield eligibility, applies overflow, and emits shield_break or recharge events.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use attributes or replicated shield state for capacity and recharge. Resolve damage filtering and overflow in Gameplay Effect execution or a combat component, then trigger Gameplay Cues for shield hits.
implementation_notes.web: Store shield_state with capacity, recharge_until_ms, coverage arc, and damage filters. Commit absorb, overflow, break stun, and recharge scheduling together during damage resolution.

relationships.reason: Shield play builds on block angle, stamina drain, and damage reduction rules with shield-specific state.
Shield defense commonly spends stamina or guard meter to limit passive turtling.
Area damage needs explicit shield coverage rules for partial overlap, facing, and splash-through cases.

example_games.title: Halo: Combat Evolved
example_games.note: Uses regenerating shields as a major combat pacing layer.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses handheld shields for blocking, durability, and parry interactions.
